ASL.description: Support and resources for American Sign Language in agent marketing activities, including interpreter services, ASL-specific marketing materials, and accessibility compliance for deaf and hard of hearing customers.
examples: Where can I find ASL interpreters for my Medicare information session?
Are there ASL-compatible marketing videos I can use with deaf clients?
How do I request an ASL interpreter for my upcoming community event?
ASL.explanation: Questions about making marketing accessible to deaf customers through ASL services, including obtaining resources, meeting compliance requirements, and arranging interpreter services.

Agent Toolkit.description: A comprehensive set of tools and resources designed to assist agents in marketing and sales activities, including templates, calculators, product guides, and presentation materials.
examples: How do I access the latest Medicare Advantage comparison charts in the Agent Toolkit?
Where can I find the enrollment checklist template in the toolkit?
Is there a premium calculator in the Agent Toolkit for the new plans?
Agent Toolkit.explanation: Questions about locating, accessing, or using specific materials or tools within the Agent Toolkit resource center.

Community Meetings.description: Planning, organizing, managing, and compliance for community-based meetings and events designed to market Medicare products to potential members.
examples: What are the guidelines for hosting a Medicare community meeting at a senior center?
How many days in advance do I need to submit my community meeting details for approval?
Can I provide refreshments at my Medicare community meeting?
Community Meetings.explanation: Questions about planning, registering, conducting, or compliance requirements for community marketing events.

Compliance.description: Ensuring all marketing efforts follow CMS regulations, state laws, and company guidelines for Medicare marketing, including material review processes and marketing activity restrictions.
examples: Is it compliant to offer gift cards at Medicare marketing events?
What disclaimers must be included on my Medicare Advantage marketing materials?
How long do I need to retain sign-in sheets from marketing events?
Compliance.explanation: Questions about regulatory requirements, limitations on marketing activities, required disclosures, or approval processes for marketing materials.

HPMS.description: Access, navigation, and utilization of the Health Plan Management System for plan information, compliance reporting, and document submissions to CMS.
examples: How do I access the HPMS system to check the latest CMS guidance?
Where in HPMS can I find the approved plan benefit details?
When will the updated plan information be available in HPMS?
HPMS.explanation: Questions about using the CMS Health Plan Management System for plan information or regulatory compliance.

Kiosk.description: Setup, maintenance, compliance, and operation of marketing kiosks in retail or community locations for Medicare sales and information distribution.
examples: What materials are allowed to be displayed at a Medicare information kiosk?
How do I get approval for setting up a kiosk at a local pharmacy?
What are the reporting requirements for kiosk marketing activities?
Kiosk.explanation: Questions about establishing or operating information kiosks in retail or public locations for Medicare marketing.

Lead.description: Generation, tracking, management, and compliance aspects of sales leads for Medicare products, including lead distribution and follow-up protocols.
examples: How do I import my leads into the CRM system?
What's the compliant timeframe for following up on Medicare leads?
Can I purchase lead lists for Medicare marketing?
Lead.explanation: Questions about obtaining, tracking, distributing, or following up on potential sales opportunities.

Retail Operations Portal.description: Online platform for managing retail store operations related to agent activities, including scheduling, resource allocation, and performance tracking.
examples: How do I update my store hours in the Retail Operations Portal?
Where in the portal can I request additional marketing materials for my store?
Can I track walk-in appointments through the Retail Operations Portal?
Retail Operations Portal.explanation: Questions about using the portal specifically designed for managing physical retail locations where Medicare products are marketed.

Sales Materials Portal.description: Platform for accessing, ordering, customizing, and tracking compliance-approved sales and marketing materials for Medicare products.
examples: How do I order additional Medicare Advantage brochures through the portal?
When will the 2026 plan year materials be available in the Sales Materials Portal?
Can I customize the enrollment packets with my contact information?
Sales Materials Portal.explanation: Questions about finding, ordering, or customizing marketing materials through the dedicated sales materials system.

Other.description: Miscellaneous sub-topics not covered in the above categories

AARP Med Supp.description: Application processes, requirements, and support specific to AARP-branded Medicare Supplement insurance plans, including standard enrollment procedures.
examples: How do I submit an AARP Medicare Supplement application electronically?
What documentation is needed for an AARP Med Supp application?
Is there an age limit for applying for AARP Medicare Supplement plans?
AARP Med Supp.explanation: Questions specifically about applying for AARP Medicare Supplement plans through standard processes, not focused on underwriting exceptions.

AARP Med Supp UW Only.description: Specialized application processes for AARP Medicare Supplement plans requiring medical underwriting, including health questionnaires and qualification criteria.
examples: What medical conditions automatically disqualify someone for Med Supp outside open enrollment?
How long does the underwriting process take for an AARP Med Supp application?
Can a client with diabetes qualify for Med Supp through underwriting?
AARP Med Supp UW Only.explanation: Questions about the medical underwriting process specifically for AARP Medicare Supplement plans, including health requirements and special circumstances.

Agent of Record.description: Processes and requirements for designating, changing, or updating the agent assigned to a policy or application, including compensation implications.
examples: How do I submit an agent of record change for an existing member?
What is the effective date of commission payments after an AOR change?
Does the client need to sign the agent of record change form?
Agent of Record.explanation: Questions about changing which agent is assigned to a policy or who receives credit/commission for an application or existing policy.

Missing Information.description: Procedures for identifying, obtaining, and submitting missing information or documentation for incomplete applications to ensure processing completion.
examples: How can I check what information is missing from my client's application?
What's the deadline to submit missing information before an application is rejected?
Can I submit missing Medicare ID information after the application is submitted?
Missing Information.explanation: Questions about resolving incomplete applications by providing additional required information or documentation.

Reinstatement.description: Guidelines, timeframes, and processes for reinstating cancelled or lapsed policies, including payment requirements and documentation needs.
examples: What's the process to reinstate a policy that lapsed due to non-payment?
How long after termination can a Medicare Advantage plan be reinstated?
Does my client need to complete a new application for reinstatement?
Reinstatement.explanation: Questions about bringing a terminated or lapsed policy back into active status through specific reinstatement procedures.

Status.description: Methods for checking, tracking, and receiving updates on the current processing status of submitted applications, including timeline expectations.
examples: How can I check the status of an application I submitted last week?
Why has my client's application been in pending status for 10 days?
When will the underwriting decision be made on my client's application?
Status.explanation: Questions about where an application is in the processing pipeline or when decisions/completion can be expected.

Withdraw/Cancel.description: Procedures and implications of withdrawing applications before approval or cancelling policies after issuance, including refund policies and documentation requirements.
examples: How do I withdraw my client's application before it's processed?
What's the process to cancel a policy during the free look period?
Will my client receive a refund if we cancel within 30 days?
Withdraw/Cancel.explanation: Questions about stopping an application from being processed or terminating a recently issued policy.

Other.description: Miscellaneous sub-topics not covered in the above categories

Election Period Verification.description: Verification of qualifying election periods that determine when individuals can enroll in, change, or disenroll from Medicare plans, including AEP, IEP, SEP, and OEP circumstances.
examples: Is my client eligible for a Special Election Period after moving to a new county?
How do I verify if someone qualifies for the 5-star Special Election Period?
When does my client's Initial Election Period begin if they're turning 65 next month?
Election Period Verification.explanation: Questions about confirming whether a client qualifies for a specific enrollment period that would allow them to make Medicare plan changes.

LIS.description: Verification processes and criteria for the Low Income Subsidy program (Extra Help), including income thresholds, application procedures, and benefit levels.
examples: How do I check if my client qualifies for Low Income Subsidy?
What documentation is needed to verify LIS eligibility?
If my client receives partial LIS, what will their copays be?
LIS.explanation: Questions about determining eligibility for Medicare's Extra Help program that reduces prescription drug costs for qualifying individuals.

Medicaid.description: Verification of Medicaid eligibility status, including dual eligibility with Medicare, application procedures, and coverage implications.
examples: How do I verify if my client has active Medicaid coverage?
What's the income limit for Medicaid eligibility in Florida?
Does QMB Medicaid status qualify my client for a Special Election Period?
Medicaid.explanation: Questions about confirming whether a client has Medicaid coverage or would qualify for Medicaid programs.

Medicare.description: Verification of Medicare eligibility, enrollment status, effective dates, and coverage details for Parts A, B, C, and D, including premium payment status.
examples: How do I verify my client's Medicare effective dates?
Is my client enrolled in Medicare Part B according to your records?
Can you check if my client has met their Part B deductible this year?
Medicare.explanation: Questions about confirming a client's enrollment status in Original Medicare (Parts A and B) or checking Medicare coverage details.

Medicare Medicaid Program.description: Verification and guidance for specialized programs serving dual-eligible individuals who qualify for both Medicare and Medicaid, including enrollment criteria and benefits coordination.
examples: Is my client eligible for the Medicare-Medicaid Alignment Initiative in Illinois?
How do I verify my client qualifies for a D-SNP plan?
What Medicare-Medicaid Program options are available for my full dual-eligible client?
Medicare Medicaid Program.explanation: Questions about specialized programs specifically designed for people who have both Medicare and Medicaid coverage.

Other.description: Miscellaneous sub-topics not covered in the above categories

Campaign.description: Information, guidance, and support for current sales campaigns, promotional initiatives, and incentive programs for Medicare products.
examples: What are the details of the current AEP enrollment incentive campaign?
When does the summer MA sales campaign begin?
How do I track my progress toward the campaign enrollment goal?
Campaign.explanation: Questions about specific time-limited sales initiatives, goals, or incentive programs designed to drive enrollments.

Communication Follow-Up.description: Guidance and protocols for following up with prospective members after initial contact, including compliant communication methods and timelines.
examples: What's the compliant way to follow up with a prospect after a marketing event?
How many times can I contact a lead who requested information?
Can I send follow-up emails to prospects who attended my webinar?
Communication Follow-Up.explanation: Questions about appropriate methods, timing, and content for communications after initial contact with potential members.

Eligibility.description: Assistance with determining eligibility for specific products or plans based on age, residency, Medicare status, and other qualifying factors.
examples: Is my client eligible for this Medicare Advantage plan if they have End-Stage Renal Disease?
Can someone enroll in this plan if they only have Medicare Part A?
Is there an age restriction for this Medicare Supplement plan?
Eligibility.explanation: Questions about whether specific individuals qualify for particular Medicare plans based on their circumstances, different from verifying official program eligibility.

How to Complete an Application.description: Step-by-step guidance on correctly completing applications for various Medicare products, including required fields, signatures, and supporting documentation.
examples: How do I properly complete Section C of the Medicare Advantage application?
What's the correct way to indicate my client wants to use autopay?
Where on the application do I enter the SOA information?
How to Complete an Application.explanation: Questions about the mechanics of correctly filling out application forms, different from questions about application status or processing.

Member Escalation-Application.description: Handling escalated member concerns or problems specifically related to applications, including submission issues, delays, and application-related complaints.
examples: My client's application has been pending for three weeks with no updates. How can we escalate this?
The system keeps rejecting my client's application due to an error. Who can help?
My client's application was submitted incorrectly by another agent. How do we resolve this?
Member Escalation-Application.explanation: Questions about resolving application-specific problems that require intervention beyond normal processes.

Member Escalation-Benefits.description: Addressing escalated issues related to member benefits, including coverage disputes, benefit explanations, and clarification of plan provisions.
examples: My client was told their physical therapy would be covered, but now they're getting bills. How do we escalate this?
The EOB shows different benefits than what's in my client's plan document. Who can resolve this?
My client's supplemental benefits aren't working when they try to use them. Can you escalate this?
Member Escalation-Benefits.explanation: Questions about resolving problems or disputes related to what benefits a plan covers or how benefits are being applied.

Member Escalation-Billing.description: Resolution paths for escalated billing issues, including incorrect premium charges, payment processing errors, and refund requests.
examples: My client has been double-billed for three months. How do we get this escalated and fixed?
My client's automatic payment wasn't processed, and now they got a termination notice. Who can help?
The billing department keeps sending bills after my client switched to Social Security deduction. How do we escalate this?
Member Escalation-Billing.explanation: Questions about resolving significant or persistent problems with premium billing or payment processing.

Member Escalation-Claim.description: Processes for escalating problematic claims, including denied claims, incorrect processing, coordination of benefits issues, and appeal support.
examples: My client's hospital claim was denied inappropriately. How do we escalate this for review?
The claim was processed with the wrong cost-sharing amount. Who can fix this urgently?
My client has been waiting 60 days for a claim decision. Can you escalate this?
Member Escalation-Claim.explanation: Questions about resolving problems with how medical or pharmacy claims have been processed or paid.

Member Escalation-ID Card.description: Expedited resolution for issues with member ID cards, including incorrect information, non-receipt, replacement needs, and urgent card requests.
examples: My client needs surgery tomorrow but never received their ID card. How do we get one immediately?
The ID card has the wrong PCP listed. How can we get this corrected urgently?
It's been 30 days since enrollment and no ID card has arrived. How do we escalate this?
Member Escalation-ID Card.explanation: Questions about resolving problems with obtaining or correcting member identification cards.

Member Escalation-Network.description: Addressing escalated concerns regarding provider networks, including network adequacy, provider participation disputes, and network directory errors.
examples: My client's doctor says they don't accept the plan, but your directory shows they're in-network. Who can resolve this?
There are no nephrologists within 50 miles in your network. How do we escalate this access issue?
My client's hospital was dropped from the network mid-year. What special rights do they have?
Member Escalation-Network.explanation: Questions about resolving significant problems related to provider participation or network adequacy.

Member Escalation-OOA.description: Handling escalated issues related to out-of-area coverage, including travel benefits, relocations, and emergency services received outside the service area.
examples: My client was charged out-of-network rates for emergency care while traveling. How do we escalate this?
My client is moving temporarily for cancer treatment but being told their coverage won't apply. Who can help?
The plan isn't covering my client's urgent care visit while they were in Florida for the winter. How do we escalate?
Member Escalation-OOA.explanation: Questions about resolving problems with coverage when members receive care outside their normal service area.

Member Escalation-Other.description: Process for escalating member issues that don't fit into standard categories, including complex situations requiring special intervention.
examples: My client has a unique situation involving hospice election and MA plan coverage. Who can help resolve this?
There seems to be a system error affecting multiple aspects of my client's coverage. How do we get this escalated?
My client's coverage was terminated due to a death report, but they're very much alive. Who can fix this urgently?
Member Escalation-Other.explanation: Questions about resolving significant member problems that don't clearly fit into other escalation categories.

Member Escalation-Prescription.description: Escalation paths for resolving prescription drug coverage issues, including prior authorization disputes, formulary exceptions, and pharmacy network problems.
examples: My client's life-saving medication was denied, and the standard appeal will take too long. How do we expedite this?
The pharmacy says my client's plan is inactive, but we have confirmation of enrollment. Who can resolve this today?
My client's tier exception request has been pending for 3 weeks. How do we escalate this?
Member Escalation-Prescription.explanation: Questions about resolving urgent or complex problems related to prescription drug coverage or access.

Member Escalation-Unable to Enroll.description: Addressing situations where standard enrollment processes have failed or been blocked, requiring special intervention to complete enrollment.
examples: The system won't let me enroll my client who has ESRD but qualifies under the new rules. Who can help?
My client's enrollment keeps getting rejected due to an error with their Medicare number. How do we escalate this?
My client is in their IEP but the system says they're not eligible to enroll. Who can override this?
Member Escalation-Unable to Enroll.explanation: Questions about resolving technical or procedural barriers preventing enrollment completion when the client should be eligible.

Plan Information.description: Assistance with detailed plan benefit information, coverage details, provider access, and plan-specific rules and policies.
examples: Does this plan cover acupuncture services?
What is the out-of-pocket maximum for this PPO plan?
How many physical therapy visits does this plan cover annually?
Plan Information.explanation: Questions seeking specific details about what a particular plan covers or how benefits work.

Provider Search.description: Assistance with locating in-network providers, checking provider participation status, and understanding provider network details.
examples: Can you check if Dr. Sarah Johnson at Westside Medical Group is in-network for this plan?
Are there any endocrinologists within 15 miles of my client's zip code?
Is Memorial Hospital and all its doctors in-network for this Medicare Advantage plan?
Provider Search.explanation: Questions about finding or verifying specific providers' participation in plan networks.

Rx Search.description: Support for prescription drug coverage inquiries, including formulary status, tier placement, cost-sharing, restrictions, and pharmacy network information.
examples: Is Eliquis covered under this plan's formulary?
What tier is Januvia on for this prescription drug plan?
Does this medication require prior authorization under this plan?
Rx Search.explanation: Questions about whether specific medications are covered, what restrictions apply, or what the cost-sharing would be.

Other.description: Miscellaneous sub-topics not covered in the above categories

AARP Med Supp.description: Information about the status of AARP Medicare Supplement applications, policies, or changes, including processing updates and policy activation confirmation.
examples: What's the status of my client's AARP Plan G application submitted last week?
Has the underwriting decision been made on my client's AARP Med Supp application?
When will my client's AARP Medicare Supplement policy be active in the system?
AARP Med Supp.explanation: Questions specifically about checking the current status of AARP Medicare Supplement applications or policies.

Authorized to Offer.description: Verification of an agent's authorization status to market and sell specific products, including certification requirements and product-specific permissions.
examples: Am I authorized to sell the AARP Medicare Supplement plans?
Has my authorization to offer Group Retiree products been approved yet?
Why does the system say I'm not authorized to offer D-SNP plans when I completed the training?
Authorized to Offer.explanation: Questions about whether an agent has the necessary permissions and certifications to sell specific products.

Certifications.description: Status of required agent certifications, including AHIP, product-specific certifications, compliance training, and continuing education requirements.
examples: Has my AHIP certification been processed in your system yet?
When will my Medicare Advantage certification show as completed?
Why is my D-SNP certification showing as incomplete when I finished all modules?
Certifications.explanation: Questions about the status of required training and certification programs that agents must complete.

Commissions.description: Information about commission payments, including payment status, calculation inquiries, missing commissions, and commission schedule details.
examples: When will commissions for last month's enrollments be paid?
Why is the commission amount for my client's plan different than expected?
Has the commission for Mrs. Smith's policy been processed yet?
Commissions.explanation: Questions about the status, amount, or timing of agent compensation payments.

Contracting.description: Status of agent contracting processes, including new contracts, contract renewals, updates to contracting information, and appointment with carriers.
examples: What's the status of my contract application submitted last week?
Has my contract been approved to sell in the new county I added?
When will my carrier appointment be completed?
Contracting.explanation: Questions about where an agent's contracting paperwork or process stands in the approval pipeline.

Demographic Update.description: Status of requested changes to agent demographic information, including address updates, contact information changes, and banking or tax information modifications.
examples: Has my address change been processed in the system yet?
When will my updated email address be reflected in the portal?
Is my banking information update complete for direct deposit?
Demographic Update.explanation: Questions about whether changes to an agent's personal or business information have been completed in systems.

OPSESC.description: Status of Operational Security requirements, clearances, and compliance elements necessary for system access and data handling permissions.
examples: Has my operational security clearance been approved yet?
When will my OPSESC certification show as completed in the system?
Why is my operational security status showing as pending when I completed all requirements?
OPSESC.explanation: Questions about the status of security-related requirements that affect an agent's system access or data handling permissions.

Premier Service.description: Status of premium service level designations, qualification progress, and special program participation for high-performing agents.
examples: Has my Premier Service status been updated for this quarter?
When will I know if I've qualified for Premier Agent status?
What's my current progress toward Premier Service qualification?
Premier Service.explanation: Questions about an agent's status or progress in special service programs designed for top-performing agents.

Prospective Agent.description: Status updates for individuals in the process of becoming contracted agents, including application progress, background checks, and onboarding steps.
examples: What's the status of my agent application?
Has my background check been completed yet?
When will I receive my agent credentials to start selling?
Prospective Agent.explanation: Questions from individuals who are not yet contracted agents but are in the process of becoming one.

Other.description: Miscellaneous sub-topics not covered in the above categories

Jarvis.description: Assistance with accessing and navigating the Jarvis system, including login issues, interface navigation, search functionality, and general system usage.
examples: I can't log into Jarvis. What should I do?
How do I access the enrollment dashboard in Jarvis?
Why does Jarvis keep timing out when I'm entering application information?
Jarvis.explanation: Questions about using the main Jarvis system platform, excluding the specific enrollment module which has its own category.

Jarvis Enroll.description: Support specifically for the enrollment module within Jarvis, including enrollment submission, application completion, and enrollment-specific functionality.
examples: How do I start a new enrollment in Jarvis Enroll?
The enrollment form in Jarvis isn't accepting the Medicare ID format. What should I do?
How do I save an enrollment application as a draft in Jarvis Enroll?
Jarvis Enroll.explanation: Questions specifically about using the enrollment functionality within Jarvis, rather than general Jarvis usage.

LEAN.description: Support for accessing and using the LEAN system, including login assistance, navigation help, and functionality guidance for this specific platform.
examples: How do I reset my LEAN password?
Where in LEAN do I find the commission reports?
Why am I getting an error when trying to submit through LEAN?
LEAN.explanation: Questions about accessing or using the LEAN system platform.

MIRA.description: Assistance with the MIRA system for Medicare enrollment and plan management, including access issues, navigation guidance, and functionality questions.
examples: How do I access the prescription drug lookup tool in MIRA?
Why does MIRA keep crashing when I try to run a plan comparison?
Where in MIRA can I find the provider network directory?
MIRA.explanation: Questions about accessing or using the MIRA system platform.

Online/Offline Enrollment.description: Support for both digital and paper enrollment processes, including electronic submission troubleshooting and paper form procedures.
examples: The online enrollment system is down. How do I submit a paper application instead?
How do I access the offline enrollment tool when I don't have internet connectivity?
What's the process for scanning and uploading paper enrollment forms?
Online/Offline Enrollment.explanation: Questions about submitting enrollments either electronically or via paper forms, including contingency processes.

SMRT.description: Help with accessing and using the SMRT tool for sales and marketing activities, including feature guidance and technical support.
examples: How do I generate a plan comparison report in the SMRT tool?
Why can't I access the drug pricing calculator in SMRT?
Where in the SMRT tool do I find the provider directory?
SMRT.explanation: Questions about accessing or using the SMRT system platform.

Technical Assistance.description: General technical support for hardware, software, connectivity, and other IT-related issues affecting agent systems and tools.
examples: My enrollment platform keeps freezing when I try to upload documents. How do I fix this?
I'm getting an SSL certificate error when trying to access the agent portal. What should I do?
Why am I unable to print enrollment forms from my laptop?
Technical Assistance.explanation: Questions about technical problems that aren't specific to a particular system but affect the agent's ability to use tools and technology.

Connected.description: Support for the Connected platform, including access issues, feature navigation, and usage guidance for this communication and collaboration tool.
examples: How do I access the message center in bConnected?
Why can't I see my team members in the bConnected directory?
How do I schedule a client follow-up in the bConnected calendar?
Connected.explanation: Questions about accessing or using the bConnected communication platform.

Other.description: Miscellaneous sub-topics not covered in the above categories

General Inquiries.description: Truly miscellaneous questions and requests that don't fit into any established category or represent novel inquiries that may require classification system updates.
examples: How do I get reimbursed for expenses when I had to drive to a client's home in a different city?
Who should I contact about an error on my W-9 tax form?
Is there a company policy on agents using their personal vehicles for business travel?
General Inquiries.explanation: Questions that don't clearly align with existing topics but still require assistance from the organization. These may indicate emerging areas that could become their own categories in the future.

Cross-departmental Issues.description: Questions or problems that span multiple functional areas, requiring coordination between different departments for complete resolution.
examples: My commissions are incorrect because my application status is wrong, and my system access isn't working to check either one. Who can help?
I'm having problems with both my certification status and marketing materials access that seem to be connected.
There's a discrepancy between what the contracting department and the commissions department are telling me about my status.
Cross-departmental Issues.explanation: Complex situations that involve multiple departments or systems and require coordinated efforts to resolve, rather than falling clearly into any single category.

Regulatory Questions.description: General questions about healthcare regulations, compliance requirements, and industry standards that aren't specifically related to marketing compliance or application processes.
examples: Can you explain how the new CMS final rule affects agent compensation structures?
What are the documentation retention requirements for agent-client interactions?
How do the recent changes to HIPAA affect my communication with clients?
Regulatory Questions.explanation: Questions about regulatory matters that are more general in nature and don't specifically relate to marketing activities or application processes already covered in other categories.

Feedback and Suggestions.description: Agent input, ideas for improvement, system enhancement requests, and general feedback about processes, tools, or support services.
examples: I have a suggestion for improving the enrollment process in Jarvis that would save agents time.
The training materials for the new products would be more effective if they included more case studies.
I'd like to submit feedback about my recent experience with the agent support line.
Feedback and Suggestions.explanation: Communications that aren't questions requiring immediate answers but rather input or suggestions aimed at improving company processes, tools, or services.

Agent Resources.description: Questions about general agent support resources, reference materials, job aids, and non-marketing educational content that supports agent activities.
examples: Where can I find the agent handbook that covers all the general policies?
Is there a glossary of Medicare terms that I can reference when talking to clients?
Are there any podcasts or webinars about Medicare industry trends that the company provides?
Agent Resources.explanation: Inquiries about general knowledge resources, educational materials, and reference tools that aren't specifically marketing materials or system-specific documentation.

Career Development.description: Questions about career advancement opportunities, mentorship programs, specialized training paths, and professional development resources for agents.
examples: What options do I have for advancing my career within the organization?
Are there any mentorship programs available for new agents?
What specialized training do I need to move into the senior markets division?
Career Development.explanation: Inquiries related to professional growth, career advancement, and skill development beyond the basic certification and authorization requirements covered in the Status category.

Meetings and Events.description: Questions about corporate meetings, agent conferences, webinars, and company events that are not specifically marketing or sales events covered under Agent Marketing.
examples: When is the next quarterly corporate update webinar for agents?
How do I register for the annual agent recognition ceremony?
Are there recordings available of the recent Medicare policy update meeting?
Meetings and Events.explanation: Inquiries about company-sponsored meetings and events that are intended for agent education, recognition, or corporate communication rather than for marketing to prospects.

Policy and Procedure.description: Questions about internal company policies, standard operating procedures, and business rules not specifically tied to marketing compliance or application processes.
examples: What's the company policy on agents using social media in a personal capacity?
What procedure should I follow if I need to take an extended leave of absence?
Is there a dress code policy for agents visiting corporate offices?
Policy and Procedure.explanation: Inquiries about general corporate policies and procedures that govern agent conduct and operations but aren't specifically related to marketing or sales activities already covered in other categories.

Technology Support.description: General technology questions related to agent equipment, software, mobile devices, and connectivity that don't specifically involve the company's proprietary systems.
examples: What are the minimum laptop specifications recommended for agents in the field?
Is there a company discount for purchasing Microsoft Office for my business?
How do I set up secure email on my smartphone for client communications?
Technology Support.explanation: Questions about general technology needs and support that aren't specific to the company's internal systems (like Jarvis, LEAN, etc.) but still impact an agent's ability to conduct business.

Other.description: Truly unique or first-time inquiries that don't align with any existing category and require special handling or the creation of new classification criteria.
examples: I've discovered what seems to be a security vulnerability in one of our systems. Who should I report this to?
There's a natural disaster affecting my area. What special protocols exist for agents in this situation?
I've been approached by a competitor to share client information. What's the proper response protocol?
Other.explanation: Rare, unusual, or unprecedented questions that don't fit any established category and may require special handling, investigation, or the development of new policies or categories.
